_comment: Mature / declining, high-payout company (illustrative) -- a legacy cash cow with flat-to-shrinking revenue. Value shifts from growth to cash RETURN and terminal decay: low/negative growth, light reinvestment, durable margin that slowly erodes. Money in $M, shares in M, rates decimals. Drives dcf_valuation.py.
_revenue: $50B base, revenue declining then flattening -- no growth story to underwrite
_margin: high but slowly eroding margin (30% -> 28%) as scale and pricing fade
_reinvest: barely reinvesting -- high sales-to-capital; cash is returned, not redeployed
_terminal: no perpetual growth; a modest ROC above a low cost of capital
_leverage: mature balance sheets carry more debt against stable cash flows
